title: Meaty Rich Braising Canadian Beef Stew
description: A hearty, crowd-pleasing stew featuring the rich flavors of Canadian beef, onions, and garlic, with a touch of spicy heat from gochugaru. Perfect for a weeknight dinner or special occasion.
instructions: Heat butter in a large kazan-cauldron over medium-high heat.
Add chopped onions and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Add stew meat and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Sprinkle gochugaru over stew meat and cook for 1 minute.
Add flour and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add beef broth, red wine, water, thyme, and salt. Stir to combine.
Bring stew to a boil, then cover kazan-cauldron and transfer to preheated oven at 300°F.
Braise stew for 2-3 hours, or until meat is tender.
Remove thyme sprigs and season stew with additional salt if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh thyme if desired.
